\section{Introduction}

Scale-free networks were first described by \cite{barabasi1999emergence}, who showed
that many real-world networks follow a power-law degree distribution.

The small-world phenomenon was demonstrated in the seminal work of
\citet{watts1998collective}, showing that most networks exhibit short path lengths
and high clustering.

% This is a comment that should be ignored \cite{ignoreme}

\section{Background}

Weak ties play a crucial role in social networks \citep{granovetter1973strength},
bridging otherwise disconnected communities.

Network science has been reviewed extensively \cite{newman2003structure,barabasi1999emergence}.

A recent study claimed surprising results \cite{fake2024nonexistent} but these
findings have not been replicated.

\section{Methods}

We use the approach described in \autocite{watts1998collective} with modifications.
